steps.title: Identify the decay factor
steps.content: The mass decreases by 18% every 9.42 minutes, so 82% remains each period. The decay factor is 0.82.

steps.title: Build the exponent
steps.content: For exponential decay with period $T$, the model is $M = M_0 \cdot b^{\frac{t}{T}}$. Here $b = 0.82$ and $T = 9.42$, giving $M = 120(0.82)^{\frac{t}{9.42}}$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $M=120(0.82)^{\frac{t}{9.42}}$: base 0.82 (82% remains) and exponent $\frac{t}{9.42}$ (number of periods). That is the correct answer.
  • Choice B — $M=120(0.82)^{t+9.42}$: exponent $t+9.42$ is not the number of periods elapsed. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — $M=120(0.18)^{\frac{t}{9.42}}$: base 0.18 would mean only 18% remains (the amount lost, not retained). Eliminated.
  • Choice D — $M=120(0.18)^{t+9.42}$: wrong base and wrong exponent form. Eliminated.
